title: New objects should not be created only to check their identity
description: This rule identifies the creation of new objects solely to check their identity.
message: New object creation for identity check.
impact: Unnecessary object creation can lead to increased memory usage.
recommendation: Avoid creating new objects just for identity checks.

noncompliant.code: a = MyClass(); if a is not MyClass: raise Exception()
noncompliant.description: Creating an object just to check its type.

compliant.code: my_instance = MyClass(); if not isinstance(my_instance, MyClass): raise Exception()
compliant.description: Checking the object's type using the `isinstance()` function.

why_is_this_an_issue: Creating new objects unnecessarily can lead to increased memory usage, slowing down the application and increasing the risk of potential memory leaks.
how_to_fix: Use existing functions like `isinstance()` to check the object's type instead of creating new objects.

logic.check_type_doc: Check if the created object's class is being compared directly for identity.

metadata.title: New objects should not be created only to check their identity
